page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn1.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 8 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing 8 vCPUs. The memory configuration consists of 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate that the ecs.sn1.xlarge exhibits lower-tier performance across several compute and memory metrics. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench tests fall into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% categories. Memory bandwidth tests show weak read performance for both small and large blocks, though large block write bandwidth reaches average levels. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3 single-threaded operations, also score in the bottom 10%. However, Passmark CPU and extended instruction tests register average performance, and LLM inference for a small 135M model achieves average speeds for both prompt processing and text generation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation provides predictable compute resources, but the overall performance-to-resource ratio is constrained by the older Intel Xeon architecture. The lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment, which may introduce additional latency and cost. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions this server for compute-focused workloads that do not require large memory footprints, though the weak benchmark results suggest it is best suited for non-critical or moderate utility tasks rather than high-throughput production environments.

Appropriate workloads for the ecs.sn1.xlarge include basic web serving, lightweight batch processing, and software compilation tasks that can utilize multiple cores without demanding high memory capacity. It is also suitable for running small-scale language model inference or utility services where baseline network throughput and moderate multi-core processing are sufficient.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn1.xlarge is an x86_64 compute-focused server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY physical cores running at 2.5 GHz and 16.0 GB of RAM. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps but contains no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data reveals generally weak performance, with single-core and multi-core CPU metrics, memory read bandwidth, and compression speeds largely falling into the bottom 25% or bottom 10% of tested servers. However, it maintains average performance in Passmark CPU tests and small-model LLM inference. Given its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, the ecs.sn1.xlarge is best suited for moderate, compute-oriented workloads such as lightweight batch processing, software compilation, and basic utility services that do not require high memory density or local storage.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn1.xlarge is an x86_64 server with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 16 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, suitable for moderate compute workloads requiring predictable CPU allocation.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn1.xlarge is an x86_64 server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon cores and 16 GB RAM, designed for moderate compute workloads.
tagline: A dedicated eight-core compute instance with a one-to-two processor-to-memory ratio for moderate, non-storage-intensive utility workloads.
bullet_points: Eight dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY cores running at 2.5 GHz
16.0 GB of RAM providing 2.0 GB per core
Baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps with KVM virtualization
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware
Average performance in Passmark CPU and small-model LLM inference
Suited for lightweight batch processing and software compilation
